{"step_by_step_thinking": "Based on the information provided in the documents, GIC (Glass Ionomer Cement) is known to have a modulus of elasticity comparable to dentin, making it similar to natural tooth structure. This characteristic allows it to provide better support under complex amalgam restorations compared to other cements like ZnPO4, ZOE, or Polycarboxylate, which do not have the same level of elasticity. Therefore, GIC is the best choice for supporting complex amalgam restorations.", "answer_choice": "A"}
